name: Basic Fern Preparation
recipeIngredient: -Lemon juice or any sauce for asparagus
recipeInstructions:
Tatum writes: "The young, tender, tightly curled fronds - croziers or fiddleheads - of bracken (or brake ferns) and of ostrich and cinnamon ferns are all prepared in the same way.
"Rub off the fuzz (or, for some ferns, the scaly coating) of the young, crisp fronds, wash them and place them, lying flat, in a saucepan or skillet with a small amount of boiling salted water. Bring the water back to a boil. Turn off the heat, cover, and simmer for 10 to 20 minutes, or until the fronds are just tender (time will depend on the size and kind of fronds)."
Season with butter, salt, and pepper to taste, or serve with lemon juice of any sauce suitable for asparagus.
